rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BONNIE WOODWORTH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar or denied without prejudice for lack of showing satisfactory progress to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 09/29/2025): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver signed by each person entitled to notice. Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must be served on each person whose request for special notice remains on file and has not been withdrawn. 3) If the estate's property on hand is $454,443, the current bond ($200,000) is insufficient, but no ex parte petition has been filed to increase the bond. If the sole beneficiary has waived the additional bond, please file a signed waiver. 4) No DE-174 form is on file in response to American Medical Response's creditor claim on file showing allowance in full. No satisfaction, release, or withdrawal signed by that creditor is on file either. 5) Please explain why the estate should be required to reimburse the attorney costs incurred for status reports #6, #7, and #9, and prior petition for final distribution ($1,340 in total) which were all taken off calendar. 6) The sole beneficiary's receipt on file did not identify each non-cash asset, in addition to a specific amount of cash, received as preliminary distribution from the estate. An amended receipt must be filed to list the assets as they exactly appear on Exhibit C to the amended petition filed on 10/19/2016, or the receipt must include a statement that each and every asset listed on Exhibit C to the amended petition filed on 10/19/2016 has been distributed. 7) Schedule of losses previously filed shows 3,308.522 shares of FPA New Income Fund #78 were sold on 08/04/2016, but Exhibit C to the amended petition filed on 10/19/2016 included the same number of shares of the same security as one of the assets for preliminary distribution in kind. 8) Schedule of gains previously filed shows 601.866 shares of Principal Inv R/E Sec-IS Fund 4934 were sold on 05/27/2015, but Exhibit C to the amended petition filed on 10/19/2016 included the same number of shares of the same security as one of the assets for preliminary distribution in kind. Moreover, inventories and appraisals on file did not list any number of shares of the same security. Did the estate acquire those shares during administration? 9) Schedule of losses previously filed shows 513.875 shares of Blackrock Total Return 332 were sold on 05/27/2016, and Exhibit C to the amended petition filed on 10/19/2016 included 2,144.612 shares of the same security as one of the assets for preliminary distribution in kind, but inventories and appraisals on file did not list any number of shares of the same security. Did the estate acquire those shares during administration? 10) The net cash amount distributable to the sole beneficiary as of a date certain, after deducting the fees, costs, and reserve sought to be approved by this petition from the estate cash. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN A. MOKRICKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The declaration filed on 2/24/2026 fails to include any of the information the court requested at the last hearing, such as reaching out to other beneficiaries/heirs about Mr. Robert J. McKee. The Court is to determine whether it will issue a final and binding order for entitlement, as Robert J's status may affect what entitlement is appropriate to the nearest generation of the predeceased spouse. Prob. Code sections 240, 6402.5,11705. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROWLEY AND LONNIE WONG REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Pursuant To Cal. Probate Code 850 A 3 A And 850 B; Request For Statement Of Decision; Objection To Hearsay And Out Of Court Evidence: And Requesy For Evidentiary Hearing And Trial
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Court to determine whether this petition needs to proceed as a quiet title action in civil court and whether the ex parte application to notice by publication should be granted. 2. Settlors' granddaughters are listed in the trust as beneficiaries, but the petition fails to state their names and last known addresses [CRC 7.902]. Notice of Hearing also has not been filed, which must be on the proper Judicial Council Form (DE-115). Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
